items.text: Unclaimed lost-and-found items will be donated or disposed of after May 30. Please visit the Student Services Office in Anderson Hall between 9:00 AM and 4:00 PM to claim your belongings. A $5 storage fee applies to electronic devices held longer than two weeks.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what will happen to unclaimed items after May 30?

options.A: They will be returned to lost-and-found storage.
options.B: They will be relocated to Anderson Hall lobby.
options.C: They will be auctioned to recover storage fees.
options.D: They will be given away or thrown out.

questions.explanation: The notice states items 'will be donated or disposed of,' which means given away or thrown out.

questions.stem: The word 'applies' in the notice is closest in meaning to
questions.explanation: In context, the storage fee 'applies' to certain devices, meaning the fee is charged for them.

items.text:
Hi Marcus,

A package arrived for you at the mailroom this afternoon. Please pick it up at the front desk in Pearson Hall before 6:00 PM on Friday. After that, packages get returned to the sender. Bring your student ID for verification.

Best,
Jordan (RA)

questions.stem: According to the email, where should Marcus pick up his package?

options.A: At the front desk of Pearson Hall.
options.B: At the campus security office in Anderson Hall.
options.C: At the package locker outside Pearson Hall.
options.D: At the residence hall front entrance.

questions.explanation: Jordan writes 'pick it up at the front desk in Pearson Hall.'

questions.stem: According to the email, what will happen if Marcus does not pick up the package?

options.A: The package will be held until Monday morning normally indoors.
options.B: The package will be moved to a secure locker.
options.C: The package will be returned to the original sender.
options.D: The package will be delivered to his dorm room.

questions.explanation: The email states 'packages get returned to the sender' after the Friday deadline.

items.text: The water fountains on the third floor of Whitmore Library will be closed for pipe maintenance from Monday, June 8, through Wednesday, June 10. Drinking water remains available on the first and second floors. Repairs are expected to conclude by 5:00 PM on Wednesday.

questions.stem: According to the notice, where are the water fountains being closed?

options.A: On every floor of Whitmore Library campus-wide.
options.B: On the third floor of Whitmore Library.
options.C: On the first and second floors of Whitmore Library.
options.D: On both basement levels of Whitmore Library.

questions.explanation: The notice specifies 'water fountains on the third floor of Whitmore Library will be closed.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred from the notice about library visitors during the closure?

options.A: They can find drinking water elsewhere in the library.
options.B: They must bring bottled water from outside the campus.
options.C: They will not be allowed to enter the library at all.
options.D: They will receive a refund of their library fees.

questions.explanation: Drinking water remains available on the first and second floors, so visitors can still get water within the same building.

items.text:
Hi Priya,

Your fall semester parking permit is ready for pickup at the Transportation Office in Hartwell Building. Please bring your ID and the $45 permit fee to complete the process. The office is open weekdays from 8:00 AM to 4:30 PM.

Thanks,
Campus Parking Services

questions.stem: According to the email, what does Priya need to bring to pick up her parking permit?

options.A: A copy of her current vehicle registration card.
options.B: A receipt from her last parking ticket.
options.C: Her ID and the $45 permit fee.
options.D: Her course schedule and a recent passport photo.

questions.explanation: The email instructs Priya to 'bring your ID and the $45 permit fee.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred from the email about the parking permit?

options.A: It can be purchased at any campus office.
options.B: It cannot be obtained during the weekend.
options.C: It is free for first-year students.
options.D: It must be renewed every academic month.

questions.explanation: The office is open only weekdays from 8:00 AM to 4:30 PM, implying the permit cannot be picked up on weekends.
